title: The Oracle (Pelgrin)
content:
{{Top|leg}}

The '''Oracle''' was an ancient and mysterious device located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pelgrin]], and was perhaps one of the oldest artifacts in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. It was created by the [[Pelgrin (species)|Pelgrin]] up to [[2,000,000 BBY|two million years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], and was known to provide intense [[Force vision/Legends|visions]] to [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]].

==Design==
The Oracle was located within an octagonal tower that stood 88 meters (ten stories) in height and was made of [[Dieuw tree]] wood, which was further supported by unknown metals. The construct possessed a complex mechanical network consisting of gears, switches, and pendulums. The Oracle itself was located deep inside a chamber within the central shaft which reached from the base of the facility to its peak. At the top of the tower was a hexagonal chamber. A spiral staircase ran up the tower.<ref name="POTJS" />

==Overview==
Popular mythology had it that [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]] personally visited the Oracle when he was at the height of his power. His return from his quest was recorded in ''[[The Despotica]]'', a collection of epic plays on the Despot's life, though given the distance of the [[Pelgrin sector/Legends|Pelgrin sector]] from [[Xim's empire]] in this period, the tale should be read with caution.<ref name="Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-history-of-xim-and-the-tion-cluster|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}}</ref>

Though it remained a mystery in modern times, the Oracle at Pelgrin was known to some in the early [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. As [[Arden Lyn]] told her [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] captors in [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]], she visited the Oracle immediately after her investment as a [[Steel Hand of Palawa|Steel Hand]] of [[Followers of Palawa|Palawa]], sometime around [[24,500 BBY]]. Lyn claimed that the Oracle's clockwork instructed her to go to the Jedi [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], where she would meet "a man fair of face and clever of mind," whom she ought to make her master. That man, [[Xendor]], together with Lyn, sought to explore a new study of the Force, leading to the [[First Great Schism|Great Jedi Schism]]. Again according to Lyn, the Jedi expunged all knowledge of the Oracle after her visit.<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

The Oracle was discovered at approximately [[3000 BBY]] by a [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] known as [[Ermin Phin-Mar]] along with an exploration team. Though it was discovered, the device was not operational at the time, but from examination, he concluded that it was created as an astronomical device. Ermin Phin-Mar also concluded that it was held by the Pelgrin people with a deep religious and symbolic nature within their culture. The [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] later dispatched [[Shannur Darkstar]] to aid the exploration team in the study of the device. It was then discovered that the Oracle actually possessed a deep connection with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] after she was meditating in the central chamber, when the machine came to life. It was deduced from this that the Oracle reacted to [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]], though there was no evidence that the Pelgrin possessed such an ability. Furthermore, the Oracle required a strong Force user to actually operate the device.<ref name="POTJS" />

It was, at this time, that the nature of the Oracle was revealed and where it gained its name as Shannur received a vision of a great battle. She was not the only one, as other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who had arrived on the planet had also received visions of the future and rumors spread that the ancient Pelgrin foresaw their own destruction long ago, even before the threat that would have caused it surfaced. However, this was simple speculation at the time as nothing indicated the fate of the Pelgrin people. Due to the powerful nature of the Oracle, the Jedi Order requested that the planet's access be restricted, which was challenged by many scientists who believed that the Jedi were attempting to keep the construct for themselves.

The Republic sided with the Jedi after several investigations were carried out on the Oracle. After that time, the Jedi maintained a log of every vision that was received and decades later those visions came true. Certain visions took millennia to occur such as the one received by Shannur Darkstar making the Oracle a treasure of the galaxy. However, tragedy would strike as the Oracle was later destroyed by pirates who were secretly working for [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] who feared that the device would reveal his plans to destroy the Jedi in the future.<ref name="POTJS" />
